steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A line with slope $m$ and y-intercept $b$ has equation $y=mx+b$. The graph shows a y-intercept of $5$, so $b=5$.

steps.title: Substitute the slope
steps.content:
The slope is $\frac{1}{4}$, so the equation is:
$$f(x)=\frac{1}{4}x+5$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice A — $f \left(x\right)=\frac{1}{4}x+5$ matches both the slope and intercept. Choice B — $f \left(x\right)=\frac{1}{4}x+\frac{1}{5}$ has the wrong intercept, Choice C — $f(x)=\frac{1}{4}x-\frac{5}{4}$ has intercept $-\frac{5}{4}$, and Choice D — $f \left(x\right)=\frac{1}{4}x-5$ has intercept $-5$. Choice A — $f \left(x\right)=\frac{1}{4}x+5$.
